_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Cervical spine fractures, instability, deformities, or infection
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Stabilization of the spine after fracture
Treatment of congenital spinal diseases (scoliosis, spondylolisthesis)
Treatment of acquired spinal diseases (scoliosis, spondylolisthesis, spinal stenosis, disk disease, inflammatory and degenerative arthritis)
Patients presenting risk factors for non-union (for odontoid fixation)
Odontoid process fracture (Type I: avulsion fracture of apex, stable)
Odontoid process fracture (Type II: fracture through base of dens, often unstable)
Odontoid process fracture (Type III: fracture extends into body of axis, usually stable)
Type IIB odontoid fracture with anterior-superior to posterior-inferior geometry (for anterior odontoid screw fixation)

preauth.label: Clinical notes with planned line of treatment
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) - affected part

claim.label: Post-procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) - affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes and X-ray clearly state Type of fracture (Simple/Compound)?

cpd_claim.q: Discharge summary rules out further complications and suggests any enhanced treatments?

common_queries: Clinical notes or X-ray do not clearly state the Type of fracture (Simple/Compound)
Pre-procedure X-ray is missing or not appropriately labelled with patient ID, date, and affected side (Left/Right)
Post-procedure X-ray is missing or not appropriately labelled with patient ID, date, and affected side (Left/Right)
Invoice and barcode for the implant used are missing at the claim stage
Discharge summary does not rule out further complications or suggest enhanced treatments

clinical_key_pointers: Metallic devices are subject to fatigue failure and may loosen or break if bone fusion does not occur, as they cannot withstand prolonged weight bearing stresses.

Odontoid process fractures are classified by Anderson and D'Alonzo into Type I (avulsion of apex, stable), Type II (fracture through base of dens, often unstable), and Type III (fracture extends into body of axis, usually stable).
surgical_options: Instrumentation for cervical spine fixation includes various types of traction (Gardner-Wells, Distraction, Halo collars), screws (cortical, cancellous, cannulated, knoringer), wires/cables (wire, songer cables), plates (occipitocervical, Caspar, H plate, Morscher cervical locking plate, Haid plate, tubular plate, malleable reconstruction plate, hook plate), and replacement materials (methylmethacrylate, structural bone grafts), and miscellaneous devices (Halifax clamps, titanium cages).

Techniques for fusion include anterior cervical fusion, Brooks, Rogers, Cloward, Yale methods, as well as specific C1-C2 fusion techniques like Gallie wiring, Magerl C1-C2 transarticular screw fixation, and Harms posterior C1 lateral mass and C2 pars screws.
Anterior odontoid screw fixation involves one or two screws inserted via the anterior-inferior corner of the C2-endplate.
